title: pugetia globularis
intro: Pugetia globularis, also known as the globularia or blue globemallow, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pugetia globularis include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The plant can be prepared as a topical cream or salve, or made into a tea by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water, making it a versatile herb for various applications.
